verses.text: Hezekiah falls mortally ill, and Isaiah bids him 'set thine house in order: for thou shalt die'; the king turns to the wall and prays with tears. He pleads with God in his sickness. Even a death-sentence is brought to the God who hears prayer.

Hezekiah falls mortally ill, and Isaiah bids him 'set thine house in order: for thou shalt die'; the king turns to the wall and prays with tears. He pleads with God in his sickness. Even a death-sentence is brought to the God who hears prayer.

Hezekiah falls mortally ill, and Isaiah bids him 'set thine house in order: for thou shalt die'; the king turns to the wall and prays with tears. He pleads with God in his sickness. Even a death-sentence is brought to the God who hears prayer.

God answers at once: 'I have heard thy prayer... I will add unto thy days fifteen years,' and deliver the city. Prayer turns the verdict. The God who appoints our times will hear the cry of His servant.

God answers at once: 'I have heard thy prayer... I will add unto thy days fifteen years,' and deliver the city. Prayer turns the verdict. The God who appoints our times will hear the cry of His servant.

God answers at once: 'I have heard thy prayer... I will add unto thy days fifteen years,' and deliver the city. Prayer turns the verdict. The God who appoints our times will hear the cry of His servant.
The sign is given: the shadow on the sundial goes back ten degrees. God reverses the sun's course. He confirms His word with a wonder fitted to the hour.
The sign is given: the shadow on the sundial goes back ten degrees. God reverses the sun's course. He confirms His word with a wonder fitted to the hour.
Hezekiah's writing recalls his despair: cut off in his prime, going to 'the gates of the grave,' his life cut like a weaver's web. Death loomed near. He felt his frailty to the full.
Hezekiah's writing recalls his despair: cut off in his prime, going to 'the gates of the grave,' his life cut like a weaver's web. Death loomed near. He felt his frailty to the full.
Hezekiah's writing recalls his despair: cut off in his prime, going to 'the gates of the grave,' his life cut like a weaver's web. Death loomed near. He felt his frailty to the full.
Hezekiah's writing recalls his despair: cut off in his prime, going to 'the gates of the grave,' his life cut like a weaver's web. Death loomed near. He felt his frailty to the full.
He had reckoned God would break his bones, yet now confesses, 'he hath both spoken... and himself hath done it.' He owns God's hand. The same God who threatened has now healed.
He had reckoned God would break his bones, yet now confesses, 'he hath both spoken... and himself hath done it.' He owns God's hand. The same God who threatened has now healed.
He had reckoned God would break his bones, yet now confesses, 'he hath both spoken... and himself hath done it.' He owns God's hand. The same God who threatened has now healed.
'By these things men live,' he says; in love God delivered his soul and 'cast all my sins behind thy back.' Healing and pardon meet. God's deepest mercy is to put away our sins out of sight.
'By these things men live,' he says; in love God delivered his soul and 'cast all my sins behind thy back.' Healing and pardon meet. God's deepest mercy is to put away our sins out of sight.
The grave cannot praise God, but the living can; 'the LORD was ready to save me,' so we will sing in His house all our days. Life is for praise. Restored health is a fresh gift to spend in worship.
The grave cannot praise God, but the living can; 'the LORD was ready to save me,' so we will sing in His house all our days. Life is for praise. Restored health is a fresh gift to spend in worship.
The grave cannot praise God, but the living can; 'the LORD was ready to save me,' so we will sing in His house all our days. Life is for praise. Restored health is a fresh gift to spend in worship.

Isaiah prescribes a poultice of figs, and Hezekiah asks for the sign that he shall go up to the house of the LORD. Means and faith go together. God heals through ordinary means, and the healed long for His courts.
